food_subgroup: Tea and infusions — catered group set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a catered multi-person tea set. FOODON_03301103 (tea beverage) is used as the closest parent category. The set includes brewed teas and accompanying pastries/snacks.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers the tea beverage component. No FoodEx2 code exists for a composite catered tea set at group-serving scale.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 covers tea as a substance. No SNOMED CT concept exists for a multi-person catered tea set product.

serving.description: Full set for 12–15 persons; per-person portion estimated at approximately 250 ml brewed tea plus accompanying snacks
serving.natural_language_portion: One realistic serving is approximately 300 g per person, comprising roughly 250 ml of brewed tea and an estimated 50 g of accompanying pastries or finger foods from the catered set.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14355); supplemented with HPB Singapore Foodo data for tea-based beverages and generic pastry components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The Signature Tea Set is a composite catered product for 12–15 persons. No single approved-source entry covers this exact product. Per-100g values are estimated from: (1) USDA SR Legacy brewed tea (NDB 14355) as the primary beverage base, weighted at approximately 83% of serving mass; (2) generic plain pastry/biscuit component estimated from USDA SR Legacy data, weighted at approximately 17% of serving mass. The blended estimate reflects a realistic per-100g nutritional profile for the composite set. Values carry medium confidence due to the composite and variable nature of the product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for plain brewed tea and biscuit/pastry components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a composite catered tea set. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate: plain brewed tea has a negligible GI contribution (GI ~1, no glycemic carbohydrates); plain sweet biscuits/pastries have a GI of approximately 55–70 (University of Sydney GI Database). The blended GI of 45 reflects the tea-dominant composition (approximately 83% tea, 17% pastry by mass). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 27.6) / 100 ≈ 12.4, rounded to 12.5. gi_category set to low per the standard classification (GI < 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content in the tea component; pastry components may contribute moderate sodium
hypertension.guidance: Brewed tea itself is very low in sodium (approximately 3 mg per 100 ml); individuals with hypertension should monitor the pastry and snack components of the set, which may contribute 100–300 mg sodium per serving depending on preparation, and should request low-sodium options where available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate and sugar content from pastry components; tea itself is glycemically neutral
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain brewed tea has no glycemic impact and may be consumed freely by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the pastry and finger food components of this catered set contribute refined carbohydrates and sugars (estimated 9.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g composite), and portion control of these items is advised to manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Tea polyphenols may confer cardiovascular benefit; saturated fat from pastry components is modest
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of brewed tea is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk due to flavonoid and polyphenol content; the pastry components contribute a small amount of saturated fat (estimated 0.4 g per 100 g composite), which is unlikely to be clinically significant at typical serving sizes, but individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit high-fat pastry items within the set.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Signature Tea Set (12–15 pax) is a premium catered tea service offered by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall, Singapore, designed for group gatherings of 12 to 15 persons. The set is priced at SGD 148 and typically comprises a selection of brewed teas — which may include premium Chinese teas, English breakfast tea, or specialty blends — alongside an assortment of finger foods, pastries, sandwiches, or traditional Singaporean kueh, depending on the menu rotation and seasonal availability. The tea component is brewed from high-quality loose-leaf or premium tea bags and served hot or iced. Nutritionally, the brewed tea itself is essentially calorie-free and contains beneficial polyphenols and flavonoids associated with antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective effects. The accompanying pastry and snack components contribute the majority of the caloric content, estimated at approximately 52 kcal per 100 g of the composite set. The glycemic index of the composite set is estimated at 45 (low GI), driven primarily by the tea base with a moderate contribution from refined pastry items. The set is suitable for social occasions, business meetings, and celebratory events in Singapore's café and premium dining culture. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake of the pastry components, while the tea itself is glycemically neutral. Sodium content is low from the tea base but may be elevated by savoury snack items. The set reflects Singapore's multicultural food culture, blending British high-tea traditions with local flavours.

confidence_reason: The Signature Tea Set is a composite catered product with variable contents depending on the restaurant's menu rotation. No single approved-source entry covers this exact product. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy brewed tea data and generic pastry component data, weighted by estimated serving composition. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than low because the estimation methodology is transparent, the component sources are approved, and the tea-dominant composition is well-characterised.
notes: 1. This is a composite catered group product (12–15 pax) with variable contents; exact nutritional composition depends on the specific items included by Kazo Premium on any given day. 2. Per-100g values are blended estimates: approximately 83% brewed tea (USDA SR Legacy NDB 14355) and 17% generic pastry/biscuit component (USDA SR Legacy). 3. Core macros are non-zero because the composite set includes pastry and snack components alongside the tea; energy_kcal 52.0, protein_g 0.8, fat_g 1.4, and carbohydrate_g 9.2 reflect the blended composite estimate. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero for brewed tea per USDA SR Legacy; pastry components do not contribute meaningful vitamin C. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero or negligible for brewed tea; pastry components are assumed to contain no industrially produced trans fats per current Singapore food regulations. 6. Fields set to -1 (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) are not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy source for brewed tea and are not reliably estimable for the variable pastry component without specific product data. 7. glycemic_index set to 45 as an analogous estimate (tea GI ~1, pastry GI ~55–70, blended at 83:17 ratio); gi_category low (GI < 55). 8. Price preserved from input: SGD 148.00 (fixed price, single restaurant: Kazo Premium – Funan). 9. Allergen flags are set to false at the dietary_flags level due to uncertainty; specific allergens are listed in the allergens array. 10. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology term exists for a composite catered multi-person tea set.
